title: Alcohols
summary: 9701 AS alcohols — classification, oxidation to carbonyls, esterification, and dehydration with interactive molecule building.
learningObjectives: Classify alcohols as primary, secondary, or tertiary.
Predict products of oxidation with acidified dichromate(VI).
Describe esterification and dehydration of alcohols.
Relate hydrogen bonding to boiling points of alcohols.

simpleExplanation.title: The –OH group
simpleExplanation.summary: Alcohols hydrogen-bond strongly, raising boiling points. Oxidation pathway depends on whether the –OH is on a primary, secondary, or tertiary carbon.
steps: Secondary alcohol → ketone only (orange → green with Cr₂O₇²⁻).
Tertiary alcohol — no reaction with dichromate under normal conditions.
Conc. H₂SO₄ + heat: dehydration to alkene (elimination).

sections.content: **Alcohols** link Topics 14–17 in organic chemistry. Distinction between **reflux** (oxidise to acid) and **distillation** (stop at aldehyde) is a classic exam trap.

sections.question: What is formed when propan-1-ol is oxidised with acidified K₂Cr₂O₷ under reflux?
sections.solution:
Propan-1-ol is **primary**.

Under reflux, oxidation continues past aldehyde:

**Propanoic acid** (CH₃CH₂COOH).

steps.caption: Alcohols contain –OH; primary, secondary, tertiary classification.
steps.embedHint: Build primary and secondary alcohols — locate the –OH carbon.

steps.caption: Oxidation: primary → aldehyde → carboxylic acid; secondary → ketone.
Tertiary alcohols resist oxidation without C–C cleavage.
steps.embedHint: Explain why tertiary alcohols give no colour change with dichromate.

steps.caption: Esterification: alcohol + carboxylic acid ⇌ ester + water (acid catalyst).
steps.embedHint: Identify the ester linkage formed from alcohol and acid.
